info.title: LingoChunk Public API
info.description:
The LingoChunk public API. Read your FSRS-graded vocabulary, look words up, browse your library, pull sliced transcripts and native audio, search your listening history, and cut audio clips.

Authenticate with a personal access token created in your account settings: send it as `Authorization: Bearer lcp_...`. Each endpoint requires a scope, granted to the token at creation.

get.description:
List the learner's vocabulary, aggregated per word, with FSRS maturity.

The per-word state is grounded in the user's real listening content. Use
``since`` (with the ``updated_at`` from a prior response) plus ``cursor`` for
incremental sync.

Incremental sync is ADDITIVE-ONLY: ``since`` surfaces new and changed words
but carries no deletion signal, so a word the user later removes will not
appear as "deleted". Clients that must stay exactly in step should do a
periodic full resync (call without ``since``) and treat that as authoritative.

**Requires scope:** `vocab:read`

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by learning status derived from FSRS state
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter by learning status derived from FSRS state

parameters.name: since

schema.description: Return only words changed at/after this ISO 8601 time (incremental sync)
schema.title: Since

parameters.description: Return only words changed at/after this ISO 8601 time (incremental sync)

parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Opaque cursor from a previous page's next_cursor
schema.title: Cursor

parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous page's next_cursor

403.description: The token lacks the scope this endpoint requires

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. The `Retry-After` response header gives the number of seconds to wait.

get.description:
Look up one word: the learner's own context first, then the shared
enrichment lexicon as a gender/CEFR fallback. 404 only when neither knows it.

**Requires scope:** `vocab:read`

403.description: The token lacks the scope this endpoint requires

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. The `Retry-After` response header gives the number of seconds to wait.

get.description:
List READY submissions in the learner's library (own + followed
collections), newest first.

Keyset-paginated on (created_at, id) via ``cursor`` (same convention as
``/vocab``). The keyset is applied in Python over the ordered rows, matching
the vocab endpoint and sidestepping SQLite datetime-comparison quirks; the
per-user library is bounded, so this stays cheap.

**Requires scope:** `content:read`

parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Opaque cursor from a previous page's next_cursor
schema.title: Cursor

parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous page's next_cursor

403.description: The token lacks the scope this endpoint requires

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. The `Retry-After` response header gives the number of seconds to wait.

get.description:
Return a submission's transcript, optionally sliced by sentence-position
range and/or time range.

Access mirrors the internal app: 404 for anything the caller cannot read.
The ``transcript_state`` field distinguishes a genuinely empty slice from a
submission that is still processing (``processing``) or ready but with no
transcript on file (``unavailable``), so a not-ready submission never looks
like an empty transcript.

**Requires scope:** `content:read`

403.description: The token lacks the scope this endpoint requires

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. The `Retry-After` response header gives the number of seconds to wait.

get.description:
Return a short-lived presigned URL to the native audio (Range-capable).

**Requires scope:** `content:read`

403.description: The token lacks the scope this endpoint requires

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. The `Retry-After` response header gives the number of seconds to wait.

get.description:
Search the caller's readable library for sentences.

``lemma`` returns the curated example sentences linked to that word - a
subset of every occurrence, but the good, hand-picked ones. The lemma is
matched case-insensitively against ``lower(lemma)`` (backed by
``ix_vocabulary_items_lemma_lower``) and joined to its sentences through the
indexed vocabulary_examples foreign keys. ``q`` does a case-insensitive
substring match on sentence text (a scan, so it is limit-capped). At least
one of the two is required.

Results are a capped SAMPLE, not an exhaustive set: there is no pagination
and no completeness guarantee. Raise ``limit`` (up to 100) for more, or use
a more specific query.

**Requires scope:** `content:read`

parameters.name: lemma

schema.description: Find example sentences for this dictionary form
schema.title: Lemma

parameters.description: Find example sentences for this dictionary form

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Case-insensitive substring match on sentence text
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring match on sentence text

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Max hits returned (a capped sample, not exhaustive)
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Max hits returned (a capped sample, not exhaustive)

403.description: The token lacks the scope this endpoint requires

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. The `Retry-After` response header gives the number of seconds to wait.

get.description:
Return a native-audio snippet of [start, end] as an audio file.

The cut is cached in object storage under
``clips/{submission_id}/{start_ms}-{end_ms}.{ext}`` and served from cache on
repeat. Rate limited per token (30/hour) AND per user (60/hour). Returns 503
when the server has no ffmpeg binary and the clip is not already cached. The
response media type follows the source audio (audio/mp4, audio/mpeg,
audio/webm, audio/wav or audio/ogg).

**Requires scope:** `content:read`

503.description: Audio clipping is unavailable on this server

403.description: The token lacks the scope this endpoint requires

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. The `Retry-After` response header gives the number of seconds to wait.

url.title: Url
url.description: Presigned URL for the full audio file

AudioUrlV1.title: AudioUrlV1
AudioUrlV1.description: A short-lived direct URL to the native audio (supports HTTP Range).

CollectionRefV1.title: CollectionRefV1
CollectionRefV1.description: The collection a piece of content belongs to.

ErrorV1.title: ErrorV1
ErrorV1.description: The uniform error body for every /api/v1 failure.

state.title: State
state.description: Most advanced state across the word's cards: new | learning | review | relearning

difficulty.title: Difficulty
difficulty.description: FSRS difficulty of the most advanced card (0-10 scale)

due.title: Due
due.description: Earliest next-review time across the word's non-new cards; null when every card is still new (a new card has no scheduled due)

reps.title: Reps
reps.description: Total reviews across the cards (all ratings)

lapses.title: Lapses
lapses.description: Total lapses (Again ratings) across the cards

last_review.title: Last Review
last_review.description: Most recent review time, or null if never reviewed

FsrsSummaryV1.title: FsrsSummaryV1
FsrsSummaryV1.description:
A learner's spaced-repetition state for one word, aggregated across all
the cards they hold for it (a word can have several card variants).

sentence_count.title: Sentence Count
sentence_count.description: Number of sentences in the transcript

word_count.title: Word Count
word_count.description: Number of words in the transcript

collection.description: Owning collection, when the item is published

LibraryItemV1.title: LibraryItemV1
LibraryItemV1.description: A ready-to-study submission in the learner's library.

next_cursor.title: Next Cursor
next_cursor.description: Pass as 'cursor' to fetch the next page; null when no more

LibraryListV1.title: LibraryListV1
LibraryListV1.description: A page of library items with an opaque continuation cursor.

SentenceSearchHitV1.title: SentenceSearchHitV1
SentenceSearchHitV1.description: A sentence matching a search, with its source and timestamps.

SentenceSearchV1.title: SentenceSearchV1
SentenceSearchV1.description:
Search results across the learner's readable library.

Results are a capped sample (see the ``limit`` parameter), not an
exhaustive set: there is no pagination and no completeness guarantee.

SubmissionRefV1.title: SubmissionRefV1
SubmissionRefV1.description: The submission a search hit came from.

text.title: Text
text.description: Sentence text in the target language

TranscriptSentenceV1.title: TranscriptSentenceV1
TranscriptSentenceV1.description: One timestamped sentence of a transcript.

transcript_state.title: Transcript State
transcript_state.description: ready = sentences returned; processing = still being transcribed (sentences empty, retry later); unavailable = no transcript will be served - either the submission FAILED (terminal, do not retry) or it is ready with no transcript data on file. Distinguishes a genuinely empty slice from 'not done yet'.

total_sentences.title: Total Sentences
total_sentences.description: Total sentences in the full transcript (before slicing)

sentences.title: Sentences
sentences.description: Sentences within the requested slice, in order

TranscriptSliceV1.title: TranscriptSliceV1
TranscriptSliceV1.description: A (possibly sliced) transcript: the sentences plus enough context to page.

pos.title: Pos
pos.description: Part of speech, e.g. NOUN, VERB, ADJ; an empty string when the source card has no part of speech recorded

translation.title: Translation
translation.description: Meaning in the learner's translation language

reading.title: Reading
reading.description: Phonetic reading (pinyin/furigana). Always null in v1; reserved for a future non-breaking fill.

card_count.title: Card Count
card_count.description: How many cards the user holds for this word

source_submission_id.title: Source Submission Id
source_submission_id.description: A submission this word was learned from (the most advanced card)

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: When any of the word's cards last changed; use as the 'since' value for the next incremental sync

VocabularyEntryV1.title: VocabularyEntryV1
VocabularyEntryV1.description:
One word in the learner's vocabulary, grounded in real listening content.

Aggregated per (lemma, part-of-speech, language): the FSRS block summarises
every card the user holds for the word, while the dictionary fields come
from the enrichment of the source submission.

next_cursor.title: Next Cursor
next_cursor.description: Pass as 'cursor' to fetch the next page; null when no more

VocabularyListV1.title: VocabularyListV1
VocabularyListV1.description: A page of vocabulary entries with an opaque continuation cursor.

pos.title: Pos
pos.description: Part of speech of the representative match (most advanced FSRS state, then card count, then alphabetical)

pos_variants.title: Pos Variants
pos_variants.description: Other parts of speech this lemma also appears under in the user's cards (the representative `pos` excluded), so nothing is hidden

translation.title: Translation
translation.description: Meaning, from the learner's own content if present

reading.title: Reading
reading.description: Phonetic reading (pinyin/furigana). Always null in v1; reserved for a future non-breaking fill.

fsrs.description: Spaced-repetition state, present only when the user has cards for this word

card_count.title: Card Count
card_count.description: How many cards the user holds for this word

source_submission_id.title: Source Submission Id
source_submission_id.description: A submission the word was learned from, if any

lexicon_source.title: Lexicon Source
lexicon_source.description: Source of the fallback gender/CEFR when the shared lexicon supplied them (e.g. 'llm', 'wiktionary')

lexicon_confidence.title: Lexicon Confidence
lexicon_confidence.description: Confidence of the shared-lexicon fallback, if used

VocabularyLookupV1.title: VocabularyLookupV1
VocabularyLookupV1.description:
A single-word lookup: the learner's own context if present, backed by the
shared enrichment lexicon as a fallback for gender/CEFR.

bearerAuth.description: Personal access token (prefix `lcp_`) created in account settings. Send as `Authorization: Bearer lcp_...`.
